key_insight: Generative Meta-Learning with Human Feedback uses expert-guided synthetic data to bridge target-domain gaps and improve generalization under distribution shift.
engineering_relevance: For materials discovery, the framework could help build robust surrogate models when real target-domain data are scarce, expensive, or partially unavailable.
method: Use a conditional Neural ODE as a generative digital twin to synthesize trajectories.
Employ a reinforcement-learning agent to adjust latent physical parameters using human feedback.
Train the meta-learner on feedback-aligned generated data to better match the target distribution.
evidence_signal: On a nonlinear Duffing oscillator, the method reduced deployment loss as expert reliability increased, and divergence between generated and target data decreased; additional results on a probabilistic model showed the approach is not limited to ODE systems.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality and reliability of human feedback, so noisy or inconsistent expert input could weaken the benefit.
paper_title: Human-Machine Collaboration on Generative Meta-Learning: Model and Algorithm
